body:
## Problem

The **Trivy filesystem scan** step in `.github/workflows/repository-hardening.yml` fails on **every PR** with:

```
Unable to resolve action `aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0`, unable to find version `0.24.0`
```

Upstream removed the old non-`v` lightweight tags; `0.24.0` no longer resolves. This is unrelated to any feature work — it's a stale action pin.

## Fix

Pin to a current, confirmed release tag:

```diff
- uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0
+ uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@v0.36.0
```

(Current tags are `v`-prefixed; `v0.36.0` is a confirmed-existing stable release.)

## Scope

One line, CI-only. Split out from #621 (Research & Targeting OS) per request so that feature PR stays focused.

## Note on the other red check

The **Gitleaks secret scan** also fails repo-wide, but for a different reason — `gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2` requires an org license stored as a `GITLEAKS_LICENSE` GitHub secret (`[Dealix-sa] is an organization. License key is required.`). That needs an org-admin secret (or swapping to a license-free scanner) and is **not** addressed here.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EMmqgsT4T39K1GqAB6uH3e

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code/session_01EMmqgsT4T39K1GqAB6uH3e)_

comments.body:
Superseded: the same Trivy pin fix (`aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0` → `@v0.36.0`) has been folded into #621 at the maintainer's request, so this standalone PR is no longer needed. Closing.

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_

commits.messageBody:
aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0 no longer resolves (old non-v tags were
removed upstream); the filesystem scan failed on every PR. Pin to the
current v-prefixed release tag v0.36.0 so the scan runs again.
commits.messageHeadline: ci: fix unresolvable Trivy action pin in repository hardening

title: ci: fix unresolvable Trivy action pin in repository hardening
